description:
About the job

Job Description

Are You Ready to Make It Happen at Mondelēz International?

Join our Mission to Lead the Future of Snacking. Make It Matter.

As an organization we are committed to developing the next generation of Makers and Bakers. Joining us at an early career stage in our fast paced and ever changing environment will enable you to do more, learn more and grow more. You will be encouraged to step outside your comfort zone– you may even surprise yourself! We will ensure you are given the support you need to be at your best and enable you to be yourself and bring passion and personality. Here you can lean in and speak up and bring your own flavor.

Innovation Excellence (50%):


Darás soporte al pipeline de innovación de la BU México: seguimiento de proyectos, actualización de maestros de datos y consolidación de KPIs del portafolio.
Construirás y mantendrás herramientas de análisis en Excel que apoyen la toma de decisiones del equipo de liderazgo.
Participarás en la preparación de revisiones ejecutivas (Portfolio Management Reviews) con materiales claros, estructurados y listos para audiencias VP.
Serás el/la guardián/a de la información: asegurarás que los datos del portafolio estén siempre limpios, completos y actualizados.



🎯 Culture & Capabilities (50%):


Apoyarás la ejecución de iniciativas de engagement y desarrollo de capacidades para el equipo de Marketing México.
Darás seguimiento a planes de acción, encuestas y programas de Champions internos, asegurando que nada se pierda en el camino.
Colaborarás en la logística y comunicaciones de talleres, trainings y actividades de cultura de equipo.
Serás el/la puente entre ideas y ejecución: estructurando procesos y dándoles vida.



💼 Gestión de recursos (transversal):


Gestionarás las órdenes de compra y el seguimiento presupuestal del área, trabajando con proveedores y herramientas internas de administración.
Llevarás el control del gasto vs. presupuesto, identificando desviaciones y alertando oportunamente al equipo.



Lo que buscamos en ti

No buscamos perfección; buscamos potencial. Pero si además tienes esto, definitivamente queremos conocerte:


Estudiante de Administración, Marketing, Economía, Negocios Internacionales o carrera afín (últimos semestres).
Mente analítica: te gusta trabajar con datos, encontrar patrones y convertir números en historias que importan.
Excel intermedio-avanzado: tablas dinámicas, fórmulas, consolidación de bases de datos. Si sabes algo de Power BI, es un plus que nos va a encantar.
Súper organizado/a: manejas múltiples tareas sin perder el hilo, cumples deadlines y tienes ojo para los detalles.
Comunicación clara y directa: sabes adaptar tu mensaje, ya sea en una presentación para el VP o en un Slack rápido al equipo.
Curiosidad y ganas de aprender: no temes hacer preguntas, proponer mejoras o dar una perspectiva fresca.
Inglés intermedio-avanzado (trabajamos con equipos globales).



No Relocation support available

Business Unit Summary

Mondelez México has been in the country since 1927 and currently employs 6,000 wonderful people. Our diverse portfolio includes iconic and mouth-watering global brands such as Trident, Oreo, Philadelphia, and local jewels like Clorets and Bubbaloo. We are leaders in the making of cream cheese, powdered beverages and confections—in fact, we make seven out of every 10 chewing gums consumed by Mexicans. Our growth is supported by our cutting-edge manufacturing facilities, such as our Puebla Plant and Nuevo León HUB, which are the largest gums, candies and biscuits factories in the world in terms of volume. You can buy are products in 900,000 places in Mexico. We are also home to one of the 11 technology centers Mondelez International has worldwide, a specialized gum and candy facility that places us at the forefront of innovation and development in the country and drives our purpose to lead the future of snacking. We are pioneers in the country in work-life balance practices such as extended maternity leave, open spaces, remote work and flexible working hours.

Mondelēz International is an equal opportunity employer and all qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, gender, sexual orientation or preference, gender identity, national origin, disability status, protected veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law.

Job Type

Intern (Fixed Term)

Interns

Early Careers

Sobre STAMP!DA

En STAMP!DA estamos revolucionando la experiencia automotriz premium en México a través de Wrap y Paint Protection Film (PPF).

Trabajamos con clientes que buscan transformar, proteger y elevar el nivel de sus vehículos mediante acabados premium, atención personalizada y una experiencia completamente diferente a la industria automotriz tradicional.

Serás la cara de STAMP!DA frente al cliente.

title: Marketing Operations Specialist
skills: Ensure data integrity of source systems to support GTM (go to market) activity by focusing on data completeness, enrichment and hygiene.
Work with the operations team to support incoming cases, ongoing process changes, and systems builds.
Benchmark and monitor campaign tracking and performance across the prospect and customer lifecycles, including MQLs, SQOs and velocity.
Collaborate with marketing operations, data and architecture teams on data hygiene, systems stability, enrichment and enhancements.
Follow through with the marketing team, ensure that you deliver projects on time and meet deadlines using project management solutions (ClickUp, Salesforce Work Manager).
Stay current with marketing automation best practices, industry standards and compliance.
Champion marketing automation best practices, including documentation, guidance, database governance and management, training, and enablement across varied teams.
Active participation in the Agile methodology to manage marketing campaign support and infrastructure improvements to support the prospect and customer lifecycles.
Use insights from platform-based reporting and new functionalities in the MarTech stack to enhance the campaign experience.
Adobe Marketo Engage certification is an asset.
We prefer experience with Marketo, Salesforce, PathFactory, Drift, Google Analytics or similar.
Degree or diploma in a relevant field.
2+ years of experience in digital marketing, marketing automation or operations.
Advanced English
Experience building and working on cross-channel marketing programs.
Data evaluation skills, with the ability to extract and interpret data from multiple sources to offer creative marketing solutions and business decisions.
Enjoy working in cross-collaborative teams and capacity to both learn from and teach others.
Strong working relationships with team members, vendors and partners, and can foster cooperation in non-hierarchical relationships.
Quick learner with the ability to rapidly correlate new concepts to existing knowledge.
Technically, and a desire to improve processes.
Thrive in a fast-paced environment, and have project management instincts, including the skills to specify project timeframes, risks and dependencies.
Comprehensive mindfulness programs with a premium membership to Calm

Employee Assistance Program

On-demand digital course library for professional development.
description:
About the job

Rockwell Automation is a global technology leader focused on helping the world’s manufacturers be more productive, sustainable, and agile. With more than 28,000 employees who make the world better every day, we know we have something special. Behind our customers - amazing companies that help feed the world, provide life-saving medicine on a global scale, and focus on clean water and green mobility - our people are energized problem solvers that take pride in how the work we do changes the world for the better.

We welcome all makers, forward thinkers, and problem solvers who are looking for a place to do their best work. And if that’s you we would love to have you join us!

Job Description

As a Marketing Operations Specialist, you will support the Commercial organization in the execution of go to market digital processes and campaigns. Have a desire to dive deep into the marketing tech stack.

Reporting to the Senior Manager, Marketing Operations, you'll work with the demand generation, SaaS marketing, and revenue operations teams. Your responsibilities will include building daily tasks, supporting campaigns, and operational programs.

Main Responsibilities


Ensure data integrity of source systems to support GTM (go to market) activity by focusing on data completeness, enrichment and hygiene.
Work with the operations team to support incoming cases, ongoing process changes, and systems builds.
Benchmark and monitor campaign tracking and performance across the prospect and customer lifecycles, including MQLs, SQOs and velocity.
Collaborate with marketing operations, data and architecture teams on data hygiene, systems stability, enrichment and enhancements.
Follow through with the marketing team, ensure that you deliver projects on time and meet deadlines using project management solutions (ClickUp, Salesforce Work Manager).
Stay current with marketing automation best practices, industry standards and compliance.
Champion marketing automation best practices, including documentation, guidance, database governance and management, training, and enablement across varied teams.
Active participation in the Agile methodology to manage marketing campaign support and infrastructure improvements to support the prospect and customer lifecycles.
Use insights from platform-based reporting and new functionalities in the MarTech stack to enhance the campaign experience.
Adobe Marketo Engage certification is an asset.
We prefer experience with Marketo, Salesforce, PathFactory, Drift, Google Analytics or similar.


The Essentials – You Will Have


Degree or diploma in a relevant field.
2+ years of experience in digital marketing, marketing automation or operations.
Advanced English


The Preferred – You Might Also Have


Experience building and working on cross-channel marketing programs.
Data evaluation skills, with the ability to extract and interpret data from multiple sources to offer creative marketing solutions and business decisions.
Enjoy working in cross-collaborative teams and capacity to both learn from and teach others.
Strong working relationships with team members, vendors and partners, and can foster cooperation in non-hierarchical relationships.
Quick learner with the ability to rapidly correlate new concepts to existing knowledge.
Technically, and a desire to improve processes.
Thrive in a fast-paced environment, and have project management instincts, including the skills to specify project timeframes, risks and dependencies.


What We Offer


Comprehensive mindfulness programs with a premium membership to Calm
Volunteer Paid Time off available after 6 months of employment for eligible employees
Company volunteer and donation matching program – We match your volunteer hours or personal cash donations to an eligible charity with a charitable donation.
Employee Assistance Program
Personalized wellbeing programs through our OnTrack program
On-demand digital course library for professional development.


We believe that employee diversity is an important element of our common future. We provide opportunities for talent growth with the entire organization. We support equality by celebrating the individuality of every person, regardless of their origin and identity. We appreciate the unique cultural pattern and variety of experiences in each of us. We invite all who want to join and change the world of our organization.

Rockwell Automation’s hybrid policy aligns that employees are expected to work at a Rockwell location at least Mondays, Tuesdays, and Thursdays unless they have a business obligation out of the office.

title: Driver Growth Operations Intern
skills: Funnel Diagnostics: Track regional user acquisition-to-activation funnels, isolating conversion drop-offs and channel efficiencies to optimize operational resources.

Cohort Analytics: Maintain performance models evaluating user lifecycles and maturity lags, running precise comparisons between treatment and control populations to isolate incremental performance gains.
Capacity Planning: Model supply capacity across regional clusters to forecast operational runway and proactively flag localized gaps before they stall expansion.
Data Pipeline Auditing: Be the primary gatekeeper for data integrity, identifying and resolving systemic anomalies between operational tools and centralized data warehouses.
Execution Tracking: Build simple compliance frameworks to evaluate field implementation quality, system utilization, and operational workflow adherence.
Process Automation: Configure automated lifecycle triggers within internal systems and leverage AI tools to eliminate manual reporting or operating loops.
Education: Enrolled in 5th–8th semester of Actuarial Science, Engineering, Computer Science, Economics, or a similarly ultra-quantitative field.
Technical Programming: Strong SQL foundations paired with data processing skills in Python (Pandas), R, or similar frameworks to manipulate and model complex datasets independently.
Automation Mindset: Proactive problem-solver with a strong interest in using tech-driven solutions and AI tools to optimize workflow efficiency.
Advanced Spreadsheets: Mastery of Excel/Google Sheets (complex logic, structured modeling) to build operational trackers from scratch.
Execution Rigor: Highly organized with the disciplined time management required to balance university schedules with fast-paced corporate sprints.
Communication: Professional fluency in both Spanish and English, with the ability to summarize raw trends into clear operational insights.
description:
About the job

About The Company

DiDi Global Inc. is the world’s leading mobility technology platform. It offers a wide range of app-based services across markets including Asia-Pacific, Latin America and Africa, including ride hailing, taxi hailing, chauffeur, hitch and other forms of shared mobility as well as auto solutions, food delivery, intra-city freight, and financial services.

DiDi provides car owners, drivers, and delivery partners with flexible work and income opportunities. It is committed to collaborating with policymakers, the taxi industry, the automobile industry and the communities to solve the world’s transportation, environmental and employment challenges through the use of AI technology and localized smart transportation innovations. DiDi strives to create better life experiences and greater social value, by building a safe, inclusive and sustainable transportation and local services ecosystem for cities of the future.

For more information, please visit: www.didiglobal.com/news

About The Team/role

Being part of the Driver Growth Operations team will give you the unique opportunity to build and optimize the supply engine of the leading mobility platform. In this role, you will help transform Driver Management into a high-impact, scalable supply capabilities model across Spanish-Speaking LatAm. Our team builds data-driven acquisition and engagement systems that structurally improve user lifecycles, boost lifetime value, and enhance platform preference.

In this role, you'll be...

As a Driver Growth Operations Intern you’ll be the analytical core for our primary scale and quality workstreams. You will maintain and optimize complex automated dashboards, monitor behavioral data patterns, and surface actionable insights needed to scale our regional operations. If you want to combine technical data skills with real-world marketplace logistics in a fast-paced corporate environment, this role offers an ideal proving ground. Your core responsibilities will include:


Funnel Diagnostics: Track regional user acquisition-to-activation funnels, isolating conversion drop-offs and channel efficiencies to optimize operational resources.
Cohort Analytics: Maintain performance models evaluating user lifecycles and maturity lags, running precise comparisons between treatment and control populations to isolate incremental performance gains.
Capacity Planning: Model supply capacity across regional clusters to forecast operational runway and proactively flag localized gaps before they stall expansion.
Data Pipeline Auditing: Be the primary gatekeeper for data integrity, identifying and resolving systemic anomalies between operational tools and centralized data warehouses.
Execution Tracking: Build simple compliance frameworks to evaluate field implementation quality, system utilization, and operational workflow adherence.
Process Automation: Configure automated lifecycle triggers within internal systems and leverage AI tools to eliminate manual reporting or operating loops.



We're eager to be in touch because you have...


Education: Enrolled in 5th–8th semester of Actuarial Science, Engineering, Computer Science, Economics, or a similarly ultra-quantitative field.
Technical Programming: Strong SQL foundations paired with data processing skills in Python (Pandas), R, or similar frameworks to manipulate and model complex datasets independently.
Automation Mindset: Proactive problem-solver with a strong interest in using tech-driven solutions and AI tools to optimize workflow efficiency.
Advanced Spreadsheets: Mastery of Excel/Google Sheets (complex logic, structured modeling) to build operational trackers from scratch.
Execution Rigor: Highly organized with the disciplined time management required to balance university schedules with fast-paced corporate sprints.
Communication: Professional fluency in both Spanish and English, with the ability to summarize raw trends into clear operational insights.



You'll love working at DiDi because...

We create user value

We strive to always create valuable experiences for our users in everything we do. Our focus is to always innovate new experiences that are safe, pleasant and efficient.

We are data-driven

We are strong believers in making informed decisions, that’s why we are data-driven. We can better navigate the business landscape strategically by analyzing valuable metrics.

Win-win Collaboration

Success is a team sport. When we work to help our partners and colleagues win, we win, too. While keeping everyone's best interest at heart, we communicate with candor and execute with excellence in all we do.

We believe in integrity

Integrity is at the very core of our business. We are people who always want to do the right thing. Our intentions are sincere, we speak our minds and listen to each other.

Growth

We always strive to do better. That means venturing beyond our comfort zones, learning from our mistakes, and helping each other grow.

Diversity and Inclusion

Diversity is one of our biggest strengths. Our differences are what make us distinct. We respect each other and believe in equal opportunities for all.

We are committed to building inclusive and diverse teams

At DiDi, we believe that our differences are our biggest source of strength. That’s why we are committed to promoting equal opportunities to all candidates and employees as an Equal Opportunity Employer.

Employment and advancement decisions at DiDi are always made based on the needs of the position and the qualifications of the candidate. We do not discriminate against any employee or applicant based on their gender, age, sexual orientation, nationality, marital status, pregnancy/maternity, disability, race, religion and beliefs, or any other status protected by applicable laws wherever we operate.

We are committed to building inclusive and diverse teams, and a workplace that is free from discrimination and harassment, because that’s how we create better products and services, make better decisions and better serve the communities we’re a part of.

description:
About the job

¡Muchas gracias por tu interés en querer formar parte de nuestra familia #GenteViva!

En Viva nuestro principal propósito es que la gente se divierta, haga negocio y se conecte con sus seres queridos aprovechando la reducción de costo en el transporte aéreo.

¡Estamos seguros que con tu talento, seguiremos volando cada vez más alto!

Como CRM & Email Marketing Analyst, serás responsable de diseñar, ejecutar y optimizar la estrategia de comunicación post booking desde CRM, con foco en la venta de ancillaries y servicios adicionales. Esta posición tiene como objetivo identificar oportunidades de negocio mediante el análisis de datos, segmentación de audiencias y diseño de iniciativas personalizadas que incrementen los ingresos complementarios y mejoren la experiencia del pasajero en su preparación para el viaje a través de los canales de CRM (email, push, in app, etc).

Funciones Principales


Diseñar journeys automatizados y campañas específicas para promover ancillaries (maletas, asientos, upgrades, etc.) y servicios complementarios después de la reserva.
Identificar tendencias, fricciones y oportunidades mediante el análisis de datos transaccionales y de comportamiento para optimizar la conversión de servicios adicionales.
Ejecutar experimentos para mejorar el performance de las campañas, identificando qué variables impactan más la conversión post booking.
Analiza datos de mercado, tendencias, comportamientos y recopila información de los clientes
Monitorear KPIs como revenue incremental, tasa de conversión por canal y por servicio, aperturas, clics y engagement, y proponer acciones con base en los hallazgos.
Crear y mantener segmentos dinámicos basados en perfil, historial, ruta, canal y tiempo hasta el vuelo para ofrecer mensajes relevantes y oportunos.


Requisitos y Habilidades


Licenciatura concluida en Marketing, Comunicación, Diseño, Mercadotecnia o carreras afines.
Experiencia de 2 a 6 años en CRM, Email Marketing y Marketing Automation.
Experiencia desarrollando estrategias de upselling, campañas automatizadas y customer journeys postcompra.
Conocimiento y manejo de herramientas de segmentación CDP (Customer Data Platform).
Experiencia utilizando herramientas de automatización.
Conocimiento de HTML para la edición y personalización de correos electrónicos.
Dominio avanzado de Microsoft Office, (Excel, Power Point, Word).
Inglés avanzado.

title: Influencer Marketing Coordinator

description:
About the job

Location: Remote (Offshore)

Employment Type: Full-Time

Experience Level: Mid-Senior (3–5+ Years)




About the Role

We're looking for an experienced Affiliate & User-Generated Content (UGC) Manager to help grow and optimize our creator and affiliate marketing programs across platforms such as TikTok, Aspire, ShopMy, and other creator commerce channels.

This is a hands-on, execution-focused role responsible for managing the day-to-day operations of affiliate partnerships and UGC production. You'll oversee creator sourcing, outreach, onboarding, gifting, affiliate link management, content coordination, performance reporting, and community engagement while ensuring a consistent pipeline of high-performing creators.

The ideal candidate is highly organized, detail-oriented, data-driven, and passionate about social commerce, influencer marketing, and creator-led growth.




Key Responsibilities

Affiliate Program Management

Manage and scale affiliate programs across TikTok, Aspire, ShopMy, and other creator platforms.
Source, evaluate, and recruit affiliate creators aligned with the brand.
Conduct creator outreach, onboarding, follow-ups, and relationship management.
Coordinate gifting, product selection, affiliate links, discount codes, and campaign communications.
Maintain organized creator databases including contact information, campaign history, product preferences, affiliate performance, and revenue tracking.
Support TikTok Shop affiliate initiatives, creator discovery, product seeding, and campaign execution.
Monitor affiliate performance including clicks, conversions, sales, revenue, commissions, engagement, and product trends.
Ensure creator content follows brand guidelines, FTC disclosure requirements, and campaign messaging.
Identify high-performing creators and recommend opportunities for long-term partnerships.
Track KPIs related to creator acquisition, activation, posting frequency, and campaign performance.
Manage creator communities by providing guidance, answering questions, sharing best practices, and maintaining documentation.
Prepare weekly and monthly reports highlighting affiliate growth, campaign performance, creator pipeline, and actionable insights.




User-Generated Content (UGC) Management

Build and manage a network of creators producing high-quality UGC for paid advertising, organic social media, eCommerce, email marketing, and product launches.
Source creators through TikTok, Instagram, creator marketplaces, and direct outreach.
Coordinate creative briefs including campaign objectives, deliverables, timelines, messaging, revisions, and usage rights.
Manage UGC production for product education, testimonials, try-ons, styling videos, unboxings, lifestyle content, and launch campaigns.
Track content deliverables, approvals, revisions, deadlines, and usage rights.
Organize creative assets for easy access across internal marketing teams.
Review submitted content for quality, brand consistency, messaging accuracy, and compliance.
Identify top-performing content formats, creators, hooks, and creative angles for future campaigns.
Deliver regular reporting on content production, asset library growth, campaign performance, and recommendations.




Qualifications

Required

3–5+ years of experience in Affiliate Marketing, Influencer Marketing, Creator Partnerships, UGC Management, or Social Commerce.
Strong experience with TikTok creator marketing and affiliate programs.
Hands-on experience using platforms such as Aspire and ShopMy.
Experience managing creator sourcing, outreach, onboarding, gifting, campaign coordination, and performance reporting.
Understanding of affiliate marketing concepts including affiliate links, discount codes, commission structures, UTM tracking, and performance analytics.
Ability to identify creators who align with both brand identity and commercial objectives.
Strong understanding of high-converting UGC and content performance.
Excellent project management and organizational skills.
Strong written communication skills for creator outreach and relationship management.
Comfortable working in a fast-paced startup or high-growth environment.
Proficiency with Google Sheets, Excel, Airtable, Notion, Asana, Slack, and other collaboration tools.




Preferred

Experience supporting US-based DTC or eCommerce brands.
Background in apparel, fashion, beauty, wellness, lifestyle, or consumer products.
Familiarity with TikTok Shop and social commerce ecosystems.




What Success Looks Like

Consistent growth in affiliate and creator partnerships.
High-quality UGC delivered on schedule.
Improved affiliate revenue and creator engagement.
Accurate campaign reporting and actionable performance insights.
Well-organized creator operations and content management systems.




Working Hours

This is a fully remote position supporting a US-based team. Candidates should have reliable internet access and be available during core US business hours for meetings, collaboration, and time-sensitive deliverables.

description:
About the job

Join Our Team at TalentPop App as an Influencer Marketing Specialist!

At TalentPop App, we're on a mission to transform the digital landscape through impactful influencer partnerships. We're looking for an innovative and strategic Influencer Marketing Specialist with strong marketing experience to help shape the future of e-commerce. If you're passionate about influencer marketing, skilled in executing high-impact campaigns, and eager to make a real impact, this is the perfect opportunity for you!

Why You'll Love This Role

You’ll be at the forefront of developing and executing marketing strategies that leverage influencers to build brand awareness, drive engagement, and boost conversions. As part of our team, your creativity, strategic thinking, and results-driven approach will directly contribute to our growth and success.

What You'll Do


Develop Strategies: Create influencer marketing plans that align with overall marketing objectives, brand goals, and measurable KPIs.
Source Talent: Identify and engage influencers who resonate with our brand, target audience, and campaign goals.
Build Partnerships: Cultivate and maintain strong, professional relationships with influencers, agencies, and brand partners.
Oversee Campaigns: Manage campaign timelines, content creation, and deliverables in collaboration with influencers and the marketing team.
Ensure Quality: Provide creative direction and review influencer content to ensure it aligns with brand identity and campaign messaging.
Analyze & Report: Use analytics tools to measure performance, generate reports, and optimize future marketing initiatives.
Collaborate Across Teams: Work closely with social media, content, and paid marketing teams to ensure campaigns integrate seamlessly into broader marketing efforts.


What We're Looking For


At least 1 year of proven experience in influencer marketing, with a solid background in broader marketing campaigns.
Exceptional relationship-building, negotiation, and communication skills.
In-depth understanding of social media platforms, influencer trends, digital marketing strategies, and audience engagement tactics.
Strong analytical skills with the ability to interpret performance data and translate insights into actionable strategies.
A creative and passionate mindset for digital marketing, brand storytelling, and campaign innovation.


Tech Requirements


Personally owned PC or laptop with sufficient processing power.
Reliable internet connection with a minimum of 25 Mbps for both upload and download speed.


Perks And Benefits


Permanent remote work setup, giving you the flexibility to work from anywhere.
HMO coverage or a health stipend to support your health and well-being.
Performance bonuses to reward your hard work and success.
Generous Paid Time Off (PTO) to rest and recharge.
Opportunities for professional growth and development within a dynamic and supportive environment at TalentPop App.


Special Instruction

For us to prioritize your application for the Influencer Marketing Specialist role, please enter code INFL25 when asked.

At TalentPop App, we’re not just hiring; we’re building a team of passionate, innovative individuals who will drive the future of e-commerce. If you’re ready to make a real impact and take your marketing career to the next level, apply now!

description:
About the job

We're a fast-growing performance marketing agency that manages tens of millions per month in direct-response ad spending for awesome direct-to-consumer brands.




We work with disruptive brands, big budgets, and make crazy ideas come to life!

Our model is built upon the core values of Alignment and Transparency, which means that, unlike traditional agencies that win by charging huge retainers, Pilothouse wins by delivering huge results.




Alongside our team of 180+ members spanning Media Buyers, Copywriters, Content Managers, Landing Page Developers, Content Creators, Email & SMS Coordinators, CGC & Influencer Managers, we need an Email and Retention Marketer Automation Specialist to own client relationships, develop email & customer retention strategies, and drive ongoing engagement and conversions, all while working alongside an amazing team.




REQUIREMENTS:

2–4 years of experience in email marketing or digital marketing.
Proven ability to manage client relationships and develop tailored email/retention strategies.
Experience building and optimizing automated email flows (welcome series, abandon cart, post-purchase, win-back, etc.).
Strong analytical skills with experience in segmentation, campaign strategy, and performance analysis.
Hands-on experience with platforms like Klaviyo, Mailchimp, and automation tools.
Ability to collaborate with cross-functional teams (copywriters, designers, technicians).
Track record of creating engaging content that aligns with brand voice and resonates with audiences.




ESSENTIAL QUALITIES:

Strategic Thinker – Can design and execute lifecycle campaigns that balance client goals with performance metrics.
Creative & Trend-Savvy – Brings fresh ideas, identifies emerging trends, and translates them into engaging campaigns.
Analytical & Data-Driven – Comfortable digging into performance metrics to generate actionable insights.
Collaborative – Skilled at acting as the hub of a multidisciplinary team and aligning everyone toward results.
Problem Solver – Able to troubleshoot issues with flows, deliverability, or campaign execution with ease.
Clear Communicator – Strong written/verbal skills for presenting strategies, insights, and results.




THE DAY-TO-DAY:

Client Management – Build and maintain strong relationships, understanding client goals and tailoring retention strategies.
Campaign Execution – Oversee end-to-end campaign creation: briefing creative, testing, scheduling, and launching campaigns.
Performance Analysis & Optimization – Monitor key KPIs (open rates, CTR, conversions) and continuously A/B test and iterate.
Segmentation & Strategy – Use customer data to create segmented, targeted campaigns for maximum engagement.
Creative Direction – Guide email copy/design to ensure high-quality output that is both on-brand and performance-driven.
Flow & Automation Management – Design, build, and continuously optimize automated lifecycle flows across the full customer journey, balancing revenue-driving automations with engagement-focused content to keep lists active.
Collaboration – Work closely with copywriters, designers, and technicians to bring campaigns to life on time and at a high standard.




ABOUT THE POSITION

This role sits on our Email & Retention team, where we focus on keeping customers engaged long after acquisition. A core part of this role involves building and refining automated email flows that drive consistent revenue while keeping engagement lists healthy. You'll act as the strategic lead for your accounts, bringing together cross-functional talent to build retention engines that drive client growth.




ABOUT PILOTHOUSE

We are focused on building a team of hustlers who are passionate about the Direct-To-Consumer space and eager to learn and grow. We push each other to do awesome work and encourage team members to bring even the wildest ideas forward.

Team culture is everything at Pilothouse. We have regular check-ins to discuss industry trends and new opportunities. You'll work with a team that has decades of marketing experience, who want to learn from you just as much as you will from them.




APPLICATION INSTRUCTIONS

Along with your resume, please submit:

A cover letter
A brief explanation of why you’re excited about this position
(Optional but awesome) An example of past email/retention campaigns you’ve worked on that you’re proud of.

We appreciate your time and thank all applicants for their interest, but we will only be responding to shortlisted applicants.




BENEFITS

Perks for our online team members

Fun virtual events — we host company events to focus on fun and growth
Casual dress
Work from home
Flexible work schedule

title: Sr Associate - Consumer Digital Ecosystem
skills: Implement a comprehensive E-Commerce strategy: design, launch, and continuously optimize Lilly’s e-commerce capabilities to drive product accessibility, sell-through, and revenue growth through digital retail channels.

Launch a telehealth solution embedded in Lilly.com: lead end-to-end implementation of a telehealth capability integrated within Lilly’s digital ecosystem to extend the reach of care, facilitate patient-physician connections, and enhance the direct-to-patient value proposition.

Build and own a clear Consumer Digital Journey: map, design, and continuously improve the end-to-end digital experience for consumers, ensuring seamless interactions across all digital touchpoints from awareness to purchase and adherence.

Own the data consumption-analysis-transformation cycle: integrate and synthesize multi-sourced data (B2B, consumer, marketing, etc.) to generate actionable commercial insights, prioritize resource allocation, and inform field and consumer strategies.

Drive commercial decision-making through digital performance analytics: monitor key digital and commercial metrics (e-commerce conversion, telehealth adoption, digital journey KPIs) and adjust strategies to maximize business impact.

Lead cross-functional collaboration: partner with brand teams, commercial operations, IT, regulatory, medical affairs, and external vendors to align the digital ecosystem strategy with broader business and compliance priorities.

Manage and scale external digital partnerships: identify and cultivate relationships with technology partners, digital health start-ups, e-commerce platforms, and telehealth providers to continuously enhance Lilly’s digital consumer ecosystem.

Support brand teams in integrating digital ecosystem capabilities into their brand strategies, including identification of digital consumer segments, definition of digital-specific Critical Success Factors, and design of brand-level digital journeys.

Stay ahead of digital trends and innovation: continuously scan the competitive and technology landscape to identify emerging digital capabilities, tools, and models that can be adopted to maintain Lilly’s leadership in consumer-facing digital excellence.
At least 3 years of relevant experience in digital marketing, commercial strategy, or digital ecosystem management, with a proven track record of driving business results through digital channels.

Hands-on experience managing e-commerce platforms, telehealth solutions, or direct-to-consumer digital channels, including ownership of key digital performance metrics (conversion rates, digital funnel KPIs).

Demonstrated ability to synthesize multi-source data (B2B, consumer, commercial, marketing) into actionable insights and prioritized business decisions, with experience in data analytics tools and dashboarding.
Experience leading multi-layered projects in cross-functional and cross-national environments, including collaboration with IT, medical affairs, regulatory, and external technology vendors.
Strong commercial acumen and understanding of how digital channels (e-commerce, telehealth, direct-to-patient) translate into revenue impact and resource allocation decisions.
Strong English skills to collaborate effectively with global counterparts and multinational stakeholders. Spanish proficiency required.

Deep understanding of the digital and multi-channel marketing landscape, including SFE models, CRM tools, marketing automation platforms, and consumer journey analytics. Experience with digital media execution and paid digital strategies.
Solid project management skills with the ability to prioritize competing demands, manage vendors and external partners, and deliver results in a fast-paced, ambiguous environment.
Some travel is possible, up to 5-15%
description:
About the job

At Lilly, the work is demanding because patients are waiting. We unite caring with discovery to help make life better for people around the world, knowing that every decision, every detail, and every day matters. Headquartered in Indianapolis, Indiana, our over 50,000 employees around the globe take on complex challenges to discover and deliver life-changing medicines, strengthen how health is understood and managed, and support the communities we serve. This is hard, urgent, selfless work—but it’s work worth doing. If you’re driven by purpose and ready to bring your best to work that truly matters for patients, we invite you to join us.

Position Title: Consumer Digital Ecosystem Senior Associate

Job Level: P2 Location: Mexico

Supervisor: Joseba Barriola Potts

Consumer Digital Ecosystem is a core commercial role within the Eli Lilly Mexico Affiliate, built to translate multi-sourced data (B2B, Consumer, Marketing, and more) into prioritized commercial decisions that drive resource allocation, field execution, and consumer strategies. The position owns the full data consumption-analysis-transformation cycle and works with internal partners (brand teams, commercial teams, global teams, subject matter experts) as well as external partners (technology vendors, digital agencies, telehealth providers, e-commerce platforms) to build and scale consumer-facing digital capabilities that directly unlock new revenue channels and strengthen direct-to-patient relationships.

The Senior Associate needs deep understanding of digital ecosystems, commercial data, and consumer behavior to couple data-driven insights with business strategy and execute high-impact digital initiatives. The Senior Associate demonstrates an innate drive to innovate, a bias for action, and the ability to create seamless consumer experiences that optimize sell-through and build lasting patient relationships.

The Position

We are seeking a data-savvy, digitally-fluent, and strategically-minded individual to join our team as a Consumer Digital Ecosystem Senior Associate. In this role, you will own everything related to the digital ecosystem that directly impacts the consumer and the business — from e-commerce and telehealth to direct-to-consumer selling models and consumer digital journeys. You will be the single dedicated resource accountable for translating data into commercial decisions that optimize digital revenue channels, enhance patient relationships, and drive sell-through across an optimized digital experience.

As a Consumer Digital Ecosystem Senior Associate, your main responsibilities will be to:


Implement a comprehensive E-Commerce strategy: design, launch, and continuously optimize Lilly’s e-commerce capabilities to drive product accessibility, sell-through, and revenue growth through digital retail channels.
Launch a telehealth solution embedded in Lilly.com: lead end-to-end implementation of a telehealth capability integrated within Lilly’s digital ecosystem to extend the reach of care, facilitate patient-physician connections, and enhance the direct-to-patient value proposition.
Build and own a clear Consumer Digital Journey: map, design, and continuously improve the end-to-end digital experience for consumers, ensuring seamless interactions across all digital touchpoints from awareness to purchase and adherence.
Own the data consumption-analysis-transformation cycle: integrate and synthesize multi-sourced data (B2B, consumer, marketing, etc.) to generate actionable commercial insights, prioritize resource allocation, and inform field and consumer strategies.
Drive commercial decision-making through digital performance analytics: monitor key digital and commercial metrics (e-commerce conversion, telehealth adoption, digital journey KPIs) and adjust strategies to maximize business impact.
Lead cross-functional collaboration: partner with brand teams, commercial operations, IT, regulatory, medical affairs, and external vendors to align the digital ecosystem strategy with broader business and compliance priorities.
Manage and scale external digital partnerships: identify and cultivate relationships with technology partners, digital health start-ups, e-commerce platforms, and telehealth providers to continuously enhance Lilly’s digital consumer ecosystem.
Support brand teams in integrating digital ecosystem capabilities into their brand strategies, including identification of digital consumer segments, definition of digital-specific Critical Success Factors, and design of brand-level digital journeys.
Stay ahead of digital trends and innovation: continuously scan the competitive and technology landscape to identify emerging digital capabilities, tools, and models that can be adopted to maintain Lilly’s leadership in consumer-facing digital excellence.



Qualifications

About You


At least 3 years of relevant experience in digital marketing, commercial strategy, or digital ecosystem management, with a proven track record of driving business results through digital channels.
Hands-on experience managing e-commerce platforms, telehealth solutions, or direct-to-consumer digital channels, including ownership of key digital performance metrics (conversion rates, digital funnel KPIs).
Demonstrated ability to synthesize multi-source data (B2B, consumer, commercial, marketing) into actionable insights and prioritized business decisions, with experience in data analytics tools and dashboarding.
Experience leading multi-layered projects in cross-functional and cross-national environments, including collaboration with IT, medical affairs, regulatory, and external technology vendors.
Strong commercial acumen and understanding of how digital channels (e-commerce, telehealth, direct-to-patient) translate into revenue impact and resource allocation decisions.
Strong English skills to collaborate effectively with global counterparts and multinational stakeholders. Spanish proficiency required.
Deep understanding of the digital and multi-channel marketing landscape, including SFE models, CRM tools, marketing automation platforms, and consumer journey analytics. Experience with digital media execution and paid digital strategies.
Solid project management skills with the ability to prioritize competing demands, manage vendors and external partners, and deliver results in a fast-paced, ambiguous environment.



Location: Mexico

Additional Information


Some travel is possible, up to 5-15%



Note: When applying internally for a position your current supervisor receives notification that you have applied to the position. We encourage employees to discuss the opportunity with their supervisor prior to applying.

Lilly does not discriminate on the basis of age, race, color, religion, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity, gender expression, national origin, protected veteran status, disability or any other legally protected status.

#WeAreLilly

Lilly is dedicated to helping individuals with disabilities to actively engage in the workforce, ensuring equal opportunities when vying for positions. If you require accommodation to submit a resume for a position at Lilly, please complete the accommodation request form (https://careers.lilly.com/us/en/workplace-accommodation) for further assistance. Please note this is for individuals to request an accommodation as part of the application process and any other correspondence will not receive a response.

Lilly does not discriminate on the basis of age, race, color, religion, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity, gender expression, national origin, protected veteran status, disability or any other legally protected status.

#WeAreLilly

title: DiDi Shop User Growth Operations Analyst

description:
About the job

About The Company

DiDi Global Inc. is the world’s leading mobility technology platform. It offers a wide range of app-based services across markets including Asia-Pacific, Latin America and Africa, including ride hailing, taxi hailing, chauffeur, hitch and other forms of shared mobility as well as auto solutions, food delivery, intra-city freight, and financial services.

DiDi provides car owners, drivers, and delivery partners with flexible work and income opportunities. It is committed to collaborating with policymakers, the taxi industry, the automobile industry and the communities to solve the world’s transportation, environmental and employment challenges through the use of AI technology and localized smart transportation innovations. DiDi strives to create better life experiences and greater social value, by building a safe, inclusive and sustainable transportation and local services ecosystem for cities of the future.

For more information, please visit: www.didiglobal.com/news

About The Team/role

The User Growth Operations Analyst role offers a unique opportunity to drive growth in one of the fastest-growing companies, specifically in an exciting & booming new business line. This data-driven position requires a blend of analytical thinking and interpersonal skills to effectively manage the user base and influence behavior.

In this role, you'll be...

What will you be doing?


Building deep data insights and understanding of our potential and existing users and retailers in the local culture, in order to build your own growth-oriented methodologies and initiatives, and also to constantly improve user experience.
Using data to understand user and merchant behavior and experience, and working closely with commercial and merchant partners to identify new opportunities for merchant growth, while always being aware of ROI.



We're eager to be in touch because you have...


 Previous operational/analytical experience in strategy operations, data analytics, consulting, investment banking, or the tech industry is a plus.
 Fluent in English.
 Data-driven with strong analytical thinking skills.
 Excellent strategic thinking and an evolving mindset.
 Self-motivated, self-directed, and eager to take ownership of tasks to ensure solutions.
 Experience in online Grocery (or Food) delivery businesses, especially in user, merchant, or category growth roles, is a nice to have.



You'll love working at DiDi because...

We create user value

We strive to always create valuable experiences for our users in everything we do. Our focus is to always innovate new experiences that are safe, pleasant and efficient.

We are data-driven

We are strong believers in making informed decisions, that’s why we are data-driven. We can better navigate the business landscape strategically by analyzing valuable metrics.

Win-win Collaboration

Success is a team sport. When we work to help our partners and colleagues win, we win, too. While keeping everyone's best interest at heart, we communicate with candor and execute with excellence in all we do.

We believe in integrity

Integrity is at the very core of our business. We are people who always want to do the right thing. Our intentions are sincere, we speak our minds and listen to each other.

Growth

We always strive to do better. That means venturing beyond our comfort zones, learning from our mistakes, and helping each other grow.

Diversity and Inclusion

Diversity is one of our biggest strengths. Our differences are what make us distinct. We respect each other and believe in equal opportunities for all.

We are committed to building inclusive and diverse teams

At DiDi, we believe that our differences are our biggest source of strength. That’s why we are committed to promoting equal opportunities to all candidates and employees as an Equal Opportunity Employer.

Employment and advancement decisions at DiDi are always made based on the needs of the position and the qualifications of the candidate. We do not discriminate against any employee or applicant based on their gender, age, sexual orientation, nationality, marital status, pregnancy/maternity, disability, race, religion and beliefs, or any other status protected by applicable laws wherever we operate.

We are committed to building inclusive and diverse teams, and a workplace that is free from discrimination and harassment, because that’s how we create better products and services, make better decisions and better serve the communities we’re a part of.

title: Marketing Manager Growth & Product
skills: Leading marketing growth and product strategy connecting the business ambition with the brand vision based on analytical and strategic thinking, including development of goals, approach, strategic definition and plan.
Bring innovative marketing ideas to the table, and best in class strategic initiatives, obtaining buy-in and efficiently implementing them at scale through marketing programs.
Business driven mindset defining strategic plans and actions to increase market share.
Use internal and external data to find relevant insights that transform into effective Brand insights, campaigns and content.
Drive research recommendations, and discussions to find sources of growth around the product, user value proposition and market expansion.
Translate high-level strategy and positioning into concrete deliverables (campaign blueprints, messaging frameworks, value propositions, etc.)
Develop marketing campaigns; providing input into media plans, key messaging RTBs and strategic briefs.
Develop campaign reporting crossing business results with brand results to find sources of growth for user value creation based on learnings and optimizations.
Develop enablement materials (Toolkits, playbooks) to scale new developments/learnings and help drive brand consistency and market penetration.
Remain fully informed on market trends and marketing brand campaigns.
6+ years of relevant experience developing marketing strategy and plan at a regional level.
Strong stakeholder management and leadership influencing teams.
Knowledge in product marketing, inapp campaigns and go to marketing strategies.
Fast learner; ability to manage competing priorities and operate effectively in a matrixed organization / fast-paced environment
Understands the “why” and applies critical thinking and strategic reasoning to decision making.
Experience in Brand and business KPIs analysis
Experience with in-app and 360 campaigns
Be able to work under constant deadline pressure and manage multiple projects.
You are fluent in English.

title: User Growth Operations Sr. Analyst
skills: Support the design and execution of integrated growth campaigns across different categories, seasonalities and user segments.
Collaborate closely with Marketing, Product and Commercial teams to implement pricing, promotion and communication strategies.
Leverage in-app surfaces such as banners, pop-ups, push notifications and CRM channels to drive user acquisition, engagement and retention.

High attention to detail and strong execution skills, with the ability to manage multiple initiatives simultaneously.
A proactive mindset, curiosity to learn and comfort working in dynamic, cross-functional teams.
Fluency in English as a working language.

title: Account Growth Executive
skills: Acelerar la etapa inicial: Contactar e impulsar a los restaurantes recién integrados a la plataforma para garantizar una adopción rápida, efectiva y de alto impacto comercial en sus primeras semanas de operación.

Venta Consultiva y Diagnóstico: Analizar el perfil de cada negocio para identificar su encaje perfecto con nuestro producto, descubrir su potencial de crecimiento y entregarles planes de acción a la medida.

Educación estratégica (Know-how): Guiar y capacitar a los socios comerciales para que comprendan a fondo el valor y las herramientas de DiDi Tu Negocio, asegurando que maximicen sus ventas y dominen la plataforma.

Gestión Comercial Dinámica: Ejecutar estrategias de contacto continuo a través de canales digitales y telefónicos, optimizando cada interacción para lograr un alto volumen de cuentas activadas y rentables de manera remota.

Monitoreo y Transición: Dar seguimiento a las métricas clave de desempeño y, una vez que el restaurante alcance su nivel de madurez óptimo y adopción total, prepararlo exitosamente para su siguiente fase dentro del ecosistema de DiDi.
Experiencia: 2 a 3 años en Customer Success (con enfoque comercial), Inside Sales o Account Management (Tech, Delivery o SaaS).
Mentalidad Ganadora: Alta motivación por cumplir y sobrepasar constantemente tus metas comerciales.
Perfil Consultivo: Habilidad para diagnosticar negocios, resolver objeciones y proponer soluciones basadas en datos.
Comunicación: Gran capacidad para conectar y persuadir a dueños y gerentes de restaurantes.
Idiomas: Inglés intermedio.
Herramientas: Manejo de CRM (Salesforce o similares) y dominio de Excel / G-Suite.
description:
About the job

About The Company

DiDi Global Inc. is the world’s leading mobility technology platform. It offers a wide range of app-based services across markets including Asia-Pacific, Latin America and Africa, including ride hailing, taxi hailing, chauffeur, hitch and other forms of shared mobility as well as auto solutions, food delivery, intra-city freight, and financial services.

Tu misión principal será actuar como el motor de crecimiento para los restaurantes que se acaban de integrar a nuestra plataforma, operando de manera estratégica, consultiva y principalmente remota.

Buscamos a un experto en éxito del cliente (Inside Sales/Costumer Success) que tome el liderazgo durante las primeras semanas de vida de los nuevos aliados. Tu objetivo será analizar a fondo su modelo de negocio, entender su máximo potencial y guiarlos para que dominen por completo el uso de la plataforma. A través de tu asesoría, asegurarás que cada nuevo restaurante traduzca su reciente registro en un despegue comercial exitoso, dejándolos encaminados, activos y listos para operar con autonomía

Gestión Comercial Dinámica: Ejecutar estrategias de contacto continuo a través de canales digitales y telefónicos, optimizando cada interacción para lograr un alto volumen de cuentas activadas y rentables de manera remota.
Monitoreo y Transición: Dar seguimiento a las métricas clave de desempeño y, una vez que el restaurante alcance su nivel de madurez óptimo y adopción total, prepararlo exitosamente para su siguiente fase dentro del ecosistema de DiDi.



We're eager to be in touch because you have...


Experiencia: 2 a 3 años en Customer Success (con enfoque comercial), Inside Sales o Account Management (Tech, Delivery o SaaS).
Mentalidad Ganadora: Alta motivación por cumplir y sobrepasar constantemente tus metas comerciales.
Perfil Consultivo: Habilidad para diagnosticar negocios, resolver objeciones y proponer soluciones basadas en datos.
Comunicación: Gran capacidad para conectar y persuadir a dueños y gerentes de restaurantes.
Idiomas: Inglés intermedio.
Herramientas: Manejo de CRM (Salesforce o similares) y dominio de Excel / G-Suite.



You'll love working at DiDi because...

We create user value

We strive to always create valuable experiences for our users in everything we do. Our focus is to always innovate new experiences that are safe, pleasant and efficient.

We are data-driven

We are strong believers in making informed decisions, that’s why we are data-driven. We can better navigate the business landscape strategically by analyzing valuable metrics.

Win-win Collaboration

Success is a team sport. When we work to help our partners and colleagues win, we win, too. While keeping everyone's best interest at heart, we communicate with candor and execute with excellence in all we do.

We believe in integrity

Integrity is at the very core of our business. We are people who always want to do the right thing. Our intentions are sincere, we speak our minds and listen to each other.

Growth

We always strive to do better. That means venturing beyond our comfort zones, learning from our mistakes, and helping each other grow.

Diversity and Inclusion

Diversity is one of our biggest strengths. Our differences are what make us distinct. We respect each other and believe in equal opportunities for all.

We are committed to building inclusive and diverse teams

At DiDi, we believe that our differences are our biggest source of strength. That’s why we are committed to promoting equal opportunities to all candidates and employees as an Equal Opportunity Employer.

Employment and advancement decisions at DiDi are always made based on the needs of the position and the qualifications of the candidate. We do not discriminate against any employee or applicant based on their gender, age, sexual orientation, nationality, marital status, pregnancy/maternity, disability, race, religion and beliefs, or any other status protected by applicable laws wherever we operate.

We are committed to building inclusive and diverse teams, and a workplace that is free from discrimination and harassment, because that’s how we create better products and services, make better decisions and better serve the communities we’re a part of.

description:
About the job

Mexico City, Hybrid

At Alcon, we are driven by the meaningful work we do to help people see brilliantly. We innovate boldly, champion progress, and act with speed as the global leader in eye care. Here, you’ll be recognized for your commitment and contributions and see your career like never before. Together, we go above and beyond to make an impact in the lives of our patients and customers. We foster an inclusive culture and are looking for diverse, talented people to join Alcon.

As a Promotional Regulatory Affairs Specialist supporting the LATAM Quality & Regulatory Affairs team, you will be trusted to manage the review process for promotional and advertising assets across Latin America while ensuring materials are aligned with approved labels, regulatory requirements, and internal compliance standards at Mexico City. In this role, a typical day will include:


 Managing projects for LATAM promotional and advertising assets, ensuring documents are reviewed, routed, and completed through the appropriate global process.
 Reviewing promotional, marketing, medical, and scientific materials for LATAM launches to identify potential compliance risks, including unsupported claims, off-label promotion, and label misalignment.
 Leading MAP meetings and partnering with Marketing, Medical, Legal, Regulatory, Franchise, and Commercial stakeholders to support clear decisions and prevent escalations.
 Supporting Brazil-related asset review by applying written Portuguese comprehension to review documents and assist the local commercial team.
 Driving process adoption and continuous improvement for LATAM promotional review workflows while maintaining accountability, documentation discipline, and compliance with SOPs and GxP expectations. 


WHAT YOU’LL BRING TO ALCON:


 Bachelor’s degree in Life Sciences, Regulatory Affairs, Pharmacy, Communications, Marketing, Business, or a related field; equivalent relevant experience may be considered.
 Minimum 4 years of relevant experience in regulatory affairs, promotional/advertising review, medical/legal/regulatory review, compliance operations, regulated content workflows, medical writing, or a related Life Sciences environment.
 Knowledge of LATAM regulatory market requirements and experience supporting cross-functional regional stakeholders; experience with Brazil-related asset review is highly valuable.
 Spanish fluency, upper-intermediate to advanced English, and Portuguese sufficient to review written documents and promotional assets; spoken Portuguese is a plus.
 Strong attention to detail, sound regulatory judgment, stakeholder management, collaboration, accountability, and the ability to find compliant alternatives that help the business move forward.
 Preferred: experience with Veeva, PromoMats, MAP or MLR workflows, quality/process/project management, SOPs, GxP, ophthalmology, or ophthalmic products. 


HOW YOU CAN THRIVE AT ALCON:


 Join a new LATAM-focused role with the opportunity to bring the regional market into a global promotional review process and help shape how work gets done.
 Work in a collaborative, international environment with exposure to Marketing, Medical, Legal, Regulatory, Commercial, and global stakeholders.
 Access a comprehensive benefits package, ongoing training, and continuous development as part of a high-performing leader in ophthalmology.

description:
About the job

Job Title: AI Engineer (Remote)

We are looking for software engineers across Latin America to join a paid remote AI training program. In this role, you will review and evaluate AI-generated code, rate AI responses for accuracy and quality, and write coding challenges to help improve AI model performance. This is a flexible, remote opportunity well-suited for software engineers seeking project-based, asynchronous work. No prior AI experience required.




Key Responsibilities

Review and evaluate code written by AI models for accuracy, efficiency, and best practices

Argentina, Bolivia, Brazil, Belize, Chile, Colombia, Costa Rica, Dominican Republic, Dominica, Ecuador, El Salvador, Guatemala, Honduras, Mexico, Nicaragua, Panama, Paraguay, Peru, Puerto Rico, Trinidad and Tobago, Uruguay, Venezuela
